name: Guava
shortDescription: A tropical fruit tree whose leaves have strong antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties.

preparation.description: Boil 5-6 fresh leaves in 2 cups of water for 10 minutes. Strain and drink warm.
Crush fresh leaves and apply directly to wounds or skin irritations.
Boil leaves, cool the decoction, and use as a gargle.

warnings: Consult a doctor before medicinal use

cultivationGuide.climate: Tropical and subtropical, tolerates brief cold
cultivationGuide.watering: Regular watering, allow soil to dry between waterings
tips: Protect from frost during winter months

research.title: Antibacterial activity of Psidium guajava leaves
research.summary: Study confirms the potent antibacterial properties of guava leaf extracts against common pathogens.

comparisonTraits.leaf: Oval-shaped, rough texture with prominent veins
comparisonTraits.smell: Distinctive, sweet, somewhat musky when crushed

name: Oregano
shortDescription: An aromatic herb with potent antibacterial properties often used for respiratory conditions.

preparation.description: Wash leaves, crush them in a clean cloth, and squeeze to extract the juice. Take 1 teaspoon.
Steep 1 tablespoon of fresh leaves in a cup of boiling water for 10 minutes. Strain and drink.
Infuse dried leaves in carrier oil (like olive oil) for several weeks. Apply topically.

warnings: Pregnant women should consult a physician before therapeutic use
May cause skin irritation in concentrated essential oil form

tips: Pinch flowers to encourage leaf growth
Do not overwater, as roots are prone to rot

research.title: Antimicrobial properties of Origanum vulgare
research.summary: Research details the high concentration of carvacrol and thymol contributing to its antimicrobial effects.

comparisonTraits.leaf: Small, spade-shaped, soft and slightly hairy
